text: Fill in the blanks about the forearm and hand.
questions: Carpal tunnel syndrome is caused by compression of the *median* nerve as it passes deep to the *flexor retinaculum* at the wrist.
Wrist drop is caused by injury to the *radial* nerve, resulting in inability to extend the wrist and fingers.
Claw hand deformity results from injury to the *ulnar* nerve, causing hyperextension at the metacarpophalangeal joints and flexion at the interphalangeal joints.
The only superficial flexor of the forearm NOT supplied by the median nerve is *flexor carpi ulnaris*, which is supplied by the *ulnar* nerve.
The anatomical *snuffbox* is a depression at the base of the thumb with the *scaphoid* bone on its floor, making it the site of tenderness in scaphoid fractures.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
Good effort — review the areas you missed.
Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
